growth_records.cite: Dillon et al. Genome-Wide Biases in the Rate and Molecular Spectrum of Spontaneous Mutations in Vibrio cholerae and Vibrio fischeri. Molecular Biology and Evolution (2017). DOI:10.1093/molbev/msw224. [PMC5854121]
growth_records.snippet: each of which was propagated for 217 days...Gen. per Line 5,187...These plates were then incubated for 24 h at 28° or 37°, and the daily generations per colony were calculated from the number of viable cells in each repr
growth_records.notes: Calculated mu from mutation-accumulation experiment data: Table 1 gives Vf-wt mean total generations per lineage = 5187 over the stated propagation period of 217 days (single-cell bottleneck restreaking, colonies incubated 24 h/cycle at 28C on TSAN). generations/day = 5187/217 = 23.90; mu (h^-1) = ln2 x generations_per_day / 24h = 0.693x23.90/24 = 0.69 h^-1; doubling_time_h = 24/generations_per_day = 1.00 h. This represents an average growth rate over an entire colony-growth cycle on solid agar (not a liquid batch growth curve), so treated as medium confidence.

growth_records.cite: Dillon et al. Genome-Wide Biases in the Rate and Molecular Spectrum of Spontaneous Mutations in Vibrio cholerae and Vibrio fischeri. Molecular Biology and Evolution (2017). DOI:10.1093/molbev/msw224. [PMC5854121]
growth_records.snippet: each of which was propagated for 43 days...Vf_mut Gen. per Line 810...generations of growth per day declined over the course of each MA experiment, particularly in the mutator lineages.
growth_records.notes: Calculated mu: Table 1 Vf-mut mean total generations per lineage = 810 over stated 43-day mutator-experiment propagation (24 h/cycle, 28C, TSAN). generations/day = 810/43 = 18.84; mu = ln2 x 18.84/24 = 0.54 h^-1; doubling_time_h = 24/18.84 = 1.27 h. Paper notes generations/day declined over the experiment (fitness cost of mutation load), so this is an experiment-average rate, not a single fixed rate; medium confidence.
